evidence_basis_summary: Authors manually scoped the corpus to TESS peer-reviewed experiments and restricted to ordinal/binary outcomes (sections 3.3, 3.1), but this is corpus curation rather than study-level relevance adjudication of admissible materials.
notes: Upgraded to medium: source addresses scoping indirectly via TESS selection and outcome-type restriction, but not a primary relevance-adjudication task

evidence_basis_summary: Paper explicitly documents that LLMs distort opinion distributions, overestimate effect sizes 2-10x, and mispredict effect directions 10-32% of the time, making human checks on causal/mechanism claims from simulated experiments a central concern (section 1).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text directly describes mechanism-claim risks that humans must constrain when using simulators

evidence_basis_summary: Paper inherits outcome constructs from the underlying TESS studies and does not build new codebooks or category schemes; construct-boundary work lives in the source experiments, not this pipeline (sections 3.1, 3.3).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms no construct boundary setting occurs here

evidence_basis_summary: Authors deliberately narrow to a single corpus (NSF TESS) with ordinal/binary outcomes only (sections 3.1, 3.3); the design goal is standardization, not corpus pluralism.
notes: Upgraded to high: full text directly shows corpus-narrowing design choice

evidence_basis_summary: Authors design the full pipeline: an LLM data-construction agent (o4-mini-high) converts TESS data to standardized persona/stimuli/response format, with documented reconstruction workflow in Appendix A, and release data, models, and code (sections 3.3, 1).
notes: Upgraded from no/low to partial/high: full text shows explicit protocol and release architecture; changed role_observable_in_case from no to partial and status from not_applicable to transformed

evidence_basis_summary: Entire section 5 is structured around transportability tests: generalization to unseen studies, unseen conditions, unseen outcomes, unseen participants, with bootstrapped Empirical Best bounds framing when predictions can travel (sections 5.2-5.5).
notes: Upgraded to high: paper is organized around transportability adjudication

evidence_basis_summary: Task is secondary-data simulation of pre-recorded TESS responses; no fieldwork, interviews, or situated interaction with respondents occurs (sections 3.1, 3.3).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms purely secondary-data design

evidence_basis_summary: Authors take responsibility for data, models, and code release with documented limitations about distributional distortion and demographic bias (sections 1, 5.6); no explicit authorship/disclosure or error-accountability framework is laid out.
notes: Upgraded to medium: implicit accountability via release and bias disclosure, but not directly thematized

evidence_basis_summary: No codebook construction or category-rule setting in this pipeline; outcomes are taken as given from TESS studies (section 3.1).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms absence

evidence_basis_summary: Paper treats demographic variance as a bias-reduction target via demographic parity difference (section 5.6), not as pluralistic subgroup disagreement to preserve.
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms the aggregation-not-pluralism framing

evidence_basis_summary: Sections 5.3-5.5 directly pressure when simulator outputs travel across studies, conditions, outcomes, and participants; gains are conditional on domain proximity and saturate around 10% pilot data (section 5.5).
notes: Upgraded to high and changed status from unclear to conditional: full text shows stage- and data-conditional transportability

evidence_basis_summary: Core contribution: Wasserstein distance distributional alignment, Empirical Best bootstrap bounds, Uniform Guess lower bounds, demographic parity difference, and SFT/DPO calibration trade-offs (sections 3.2, 5.2, 5.6).
notes: Upgraded to high and strengthened status from conditional to robust: calibration is the paper's central human-designed work

evidence_basis_summary: No interviewer or fieldworker role; responses are drawn from pre-existing TESS survey data (section 3.3).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms absence

evidence_basis_summary: No tacit, archival, or contextual interpretive work beyond the automated reconstruction agent; questions are standardized text (section 3.3).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms absence

evidence_basis_summary: Paper explicitly positions simulators for hypothesis screening before costly studies and pilot generalization to unseen participants, with saturation around 10% pilot data (sections 1, 5.5).
notes: Upgraded to high and changed role_observable_in_case from no to partial and status to shrinking: hypothesis-screening framing is the paper's motivating use case

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Task is response prediction against ground-truth human responses, not labeling or coding; no coder audit-vs-primary shift is described (sections 3.1-3.2).
notes: Upgraded to high: full text confirms this is prediction, not coding
